name: AZQ Stage 3 Wave A Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 3 Wave A so the Codex runner can evaluate the Agenda storage-and-schema foundation work without hardcoding Stage 3 logic into the runner.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the Wave A patch.

baseline_checks.name: existing_regression_suite
baseline_checks.description: Verify the existing Stage 1 and Stage 2 regression suite still passes.

baseline_checks.name: agenda_storage_import
baseline_checks.description: Verify the Agenda storage facade imports cleanly.

STAGE3-WAVEA-01.description: Create Agenda package scaffolding and storage facade.

checks.name: agenda_package_files_exist
checks.description: Verify the Agenda package and storage facade files exist.

checks.name: agenda_package_imports_cleanly
checks.description: Verify the new Agenda package imports without error.

human_checks: The initial Agenda storage facade stays small and import-safe rather than becoming an implementation blob immediately.
No Agenda CLI command behavior was introduced in this scaffolding task.

STAGE3-WAVEA-02.description: Add canonical Agenda path constants and directory helpers.

checks.name: agenda_paths_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the dedicated Agenda paths module exists.

checks.name: agenda_paths_helpers_exist
checks.description: Verify the Agenda paths module exposes the expected canonical path helpers.

checks.name: agenda_paths_helpers_behave
checks.description: Verify the Agenda path helpers create canonical directories and resolve deterministic file paths.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.agenda import paths
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    tmp_path = Path(tmp)
    paths.DATA_DIR = tmp_path / 'data'
    paths.AGENDA_DIR = paths.DATA_DIR / 'agenda'
    paths.TASKS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'tasks'
    paths.DAGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'dags'
    paths.LOGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'logs'
    paths.ensure_agenda_dirs()
    assert paths.TASKS_DIR.is_dir()
    assert paths.DAGS_DIR.is_dir()
    assert paths.LOGS_DIR.is_dir()
    assert paths.task_file_path('TASK_001').name == 'TASK_001.md'
    assert paths.dag_file_path('FINIS_001').name == 'GOAL_FINIS_001_DAG.json'
    assert paths.task_log_file_path('TASK_001').name == 'TASK_001_LOG.md'

human_checks: Path ownership lives in the focused paths module rather than being scattered through storage helpers.
The canonical Stage 3 directories remain explicit and inspectable.

STAGE3-WAVEA-03.description: Define the canonical normalized task-record schema.

checks.name: agenda_task_schema_helper_exists
checks.description: Verify the Agenda schema module exposes a task normalization helper.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import schemas
assert hasattr(schemas, 'normalize_task_record') or hasattr(schemas, 'normalize_task'), 'missing task normalization helper'

checks.name: agenda_task_schema_normalizes_required_fields
checks.description: Verify a partial Agenda task record normalizes into the canonical Stage 3 task schema.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import schemas
helper = getattr(schemas, 'normalize_task_record', None) or getattr(schemas, 'normalize_task', None)
record = helper({'task_id': 'TASK_001', 'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'description': 'First task'})
for field in ['task_id', 'deliverable_id', 'description', 'dependencies', 'status', 'execution_notes', 'created']:
    assert field in record, field
assert record['task_id'] == 'TASK_001'
assert record['deliverable_id'] == 'DELIV_001'
assert isinstance(record['dependencies'], list)

human_checks: Task normalization stays separate from DAG normalization.
The normalized task record remains deliverable-centered rather than drifting into later command behavior.

STAGE3-WAVEA-04.description: Implement markdown task-record serialization, parsing, and repository reads.

checks.name: agenda_task_storage_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the dedicated Agenda task-storage module exists.

checks.name: agenda_task_storage_helpers_exist
checks.description: Verify the task-storage module exposes the expected canonical task helpers.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import task_storage
for name in ['serialize_task_record', 'parse_task_record', 'load_all_tasks', 'load_task', 'next_task_id']:
    assert hasattr(task_storage, name), name
assert hasattr(task_storage, 'load_tasks_for_deliverable') or hasattr(task_storage, 'load_deliverable_tasks')
assert hasattr(task_storage, 'write_task') or hasattr(task_storage, 'save_task')

checks.name: agenda_task_round_trip
checks.description: Verify a canonical Agenda task record can round-trip through markdown serialization and parsing.

checks.name: agenda_task_repository_helpers_behave
checks.description: Verify Agenda task repository helpers can write, read, group, and allocate task ids from canonical task files using the shared path layer.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.agenda import paths, task_storage
record_1 = {'task_id': 'TASK_001', 'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'description': 'First task', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'ready', 'execution_notes': '', 'created': '2026-03-18'}
record_2 = {'task_id': 'TASK_002', 'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'description': 'Second task', 'dependencies': ['TASK_001'], 'status': 'ready', 'execution_notes': '', 'created': '2026-03-18'}
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    tmp_path = Path(tmp)
    paths.DATA_DIR = tmp_path / 'data'
    paths.AGENDA_DIR = paths.DATA_DIR / 'agenda'
    paths.TASKS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'tasks'
    paths.DAGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'dags'
    paths.LOGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'logs'
    paths.ensure_agenda_dirs()
    write_helper = getattr(task_storage, 'write_task', None) or getattr(task_storage, 'save_task', None)
    assert write_helper is not None, 'missing task write helper'
    write_helper(record_1)
    write_helper(record_2)
    assert task_storage.load_task('TASK_001')['description'] == 'First task'
    assert [item['task_id'] for item in task_storage.load_all_tasks()] == ['TASK_001', 'TASK_002']
    group_helper = getattr(task_storage, 'load_tasks_for_deliverable', None) or getattr(task_storage, 'load_deliverable_tasks', None)
    assert group_helper is not None, 'missing deliverable task grouping helper'
    assert [item['task_id'] for item in group_helper('DELIV_001')] == ['TASK_001', 'TASK_002']
    assert task_storage.next_task_id() == 'TASK_003'

human_checks: Task storage stays in a dedicated module rather than growing the public facade into a grab bag.
The task-record format is readable and diff-friendly from the filesystem.

STAGE3-WAVEA-05.description: Add canonical deliverable-parent validation for Agenda tasks.

checks.name: agenda_deliverable_validation_helper_exists
checks.description: Verify Agenda exposes a deliverable-parent validation helper.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import task_storage
assert hasattr(task_storage, 'validate_parent_deliverable') or hasattr(task_storage, 'validate_deliverable_parent'), 'missing deliverable validation helper'

checks.name: agenda_deliverable_validation_checks_exact_parent
checks.description: Verify Agenda deliverable-parent validation succeeds for a present canonical deliverable and fails for a missing one.
checks.code:
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.agenda import task_storage
helper = getattr(task_storage, 'validate_parent_deliverable', None) or getattr(task_storage, 'validate_deliverable_parent', None)
assert helper is not None, 'missing deliverable validation helper'
with patch('azq.formam.storage.load_deliverable', return_value={'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Parent deliverable', 'artifact_description': '', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'drafted', 'created': ''}):
    result = helper('DELIV_001')
    assert result['deliverable_id'] == 'DELIV_001'
with patch('azq.formam.storage.load_deliverable', return_value=None):
    try:
        helper('DELIV_999')
    except Exception:
        pass
    else:
        raise AssertionError('missing deliverable should fail validation')

human_checks: Deliverable-parent validation should use the Formam storage boundary rather than recreating deliverable truth inside Agenda.
Missing or malformed parent deliverables should fail clearly before any later Wave B task creation behavior occurs.

STAGE3-WAVEA-06.description: Define the canonical normalized task-DAG schema.

checks.name: agenda_dag_schema_helper_exists
checks.description: Verify the Agenda schema module exposes a DAG normalization helper.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import schemas
assert hasattr(schemas, 'normalize_dag_record') or hasattr(schemas, 'normalize_task_dag_record') or hasattr(schemas, 'normalize_dag'), 'missing DAG normalization helper'

checks.name: agenda_dag_schema_normalizes_required_fields
checks.description: Verify a partial Agenda DAG record normalizes into the canonical Stage 3 DAG schema.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import schemas
helper = getattr(schemas, 'normalize_dag_record', None) or getattr(schemas, 'normalize_task_dag_record', None) or getattr(schemas, 'normalize_dag', None)
record = helper({'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'deliverable_ids': ['DELIV_001']})
for field in ['goal_id', 'deliverable_ids', 'task_ids', 'dependency_edges', 'status', 'created', 'notes']:
    assert field in record, field
assert record['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
assert isinstance(record['deliverable_ids'], list)
assert isinstance(record['task_ids'], list)
assert isinstance(record['dependency_edges'], list)

human_checks: DAG normalization remains distinct from task-record normalization.
The DAG schema stays sparse and inspectable rather than pretending to be a smart planner.

STAGE3-WAVEA-07.description: Implement canonical DAG artifact serialization and repository helpers.

checks.name: agenda_dag_storage_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the dedicated Agenda DAG-storage module exists.

checks.name: agenda_dag_storage_helpers_exist
checks.description: Verify the DAG-storage module exposes canonical DAG read, write, and round-trip helpers.

checks.name: agenda_dag_round_trip
checks.description: Verify a canonical Agenda DAG record can round-trip through the DAG serialization helpers.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import dag_storage
record = {'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'deliverable_ids': ['DELIV_001'], 'task_ids': ['TASK_001'], 'dependency_edges': [{'from': 'TASK_001', 'to': 'TASK_002'}], 'status': 'draft', 'created': '2026-03-18', 'notes': 'Visible graph'}
text = dag_storage.serialize_dag_record(record)
parsed = dag_storage.parse_dag_record(text)
for field in ['goal_id', 'deliverable_ids', 'task_ids', 'dependency_edges', 'status', 'created', 'notes']:
    assert field in parsed, field
assert parsed['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'

checks.name: agenda_dag_repository_helpers_behave
checks.description: Verify Agenda DAG storage helpers can write and read one canonical DAG artifact using the shared path layer.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.agenda import paths, dag_storage
record = {'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'deliverable_ids': ['DELIV_001'], 'task_ids': ['TASK_001'], 'dependency_edges': [], 'status': 'draft', 'created': '2026-03-18', 'notes': ''}
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    tmp_path = Path(tmp)
    paths.DATA_DIR = tmp_path / 'data'
    paths.AGENDA_DIR = paths.DATA_DIR / 'agenda'
    paths.TASKS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'tasks'
    paths.DAGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'dags'
    paths.LOGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'logs'
    paths.ensure_agenda_dirs()
    path = dag_storage.write_dag(record)
    assert path.is_file(), 'dag file was not written'
    assert path.name == 'GOAL_FINIS_001_DAG.json'
    loaded = dag_storage.load_dag('FINIS_001')
    assert loaded['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
    assert loaded['deliverable_ids'] == ['DELIV_001']

human_checks: DAG storage logic stays in a focused module rather than being mixed into task-record storage.
Any dependency-edge derivation logic belongs with DAG storage or DAG shaping, not in the general storage facade.

checks.name: agenda_log_storage_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the dedicated Agenda log-storage module exists.

checks.name: agenda_log_storage_helpers_exist
checks.description: Verify the log-storage module exposes canonical task-log helpers.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import log_storage
assert hasattr(log_storage, 'task_log_file_path') or hasattr(log_storage, 'log_file_path')
assert hasattr(log_storage, 'write_task_log') or hasattr(log_storage, 'create_task_log') or hasattr(log_storage, 'append_task_log_entry')

checks.name: agenda_log_storage_writes_visible_artifact
checks.description: Verify Agenda log storage can create a visible task-local log artifact in the canonical logs directory through the shared path layer.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.agenda import paths, log_storage
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    tmp_path = Path(tmp)
    paths.DATA_DIR = tmp_path / 'data'
    paths.AGENDA_DIR = paths.DATA_DIR / 'agenda'
    paths.TASKS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'tasks'
    paths.DAGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'dags'
    paths.LOGS_DIR = paths.AGENDA_DIR / 'logs'
    paths.ensure_agenda_dirs()
    helper = getattr(log_storage, 'write_task_log', None) or getattr(log_storage, 'create_task_log', None) or getattr(log_storage, 'append_task_log_entry', None)
    assert helper is not None, 'missing log write helper'
    try:
        helper('TASK_001', 'started', 'Initial log entry')
    except TypeError:
        helper({'task_id': 'TASK_001'}, 'started', 'Initial log entry')
    path_helper = getattr(log_storage, 'task_log_file_path', None) or getattr(log_storage, 'log_file_path', None)
    assert path_helper is not None, 'missing log path helper'
    log_path = path_helper('TASK_001')
    assert log_path.is_file(), 'task log file was not written'
    text = log_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    assert 'TASK_001' in text or 'Initial log entry' in text

human_checks: Task-log storage should remain a supporting artifact layer rather than becoming the primary source of task truth.
Wave A log helpers should stay separate from execution orchestration and later artifact/archive behavior.

STAGE3-WAVEA-09.description: Reduce azq.agenda.storage to a thin public facade.

checks.name: agenda_storage_facade_exposes_wave_a_surface
checks.description: Verify the Agenda storage facade exposes the public Wave A helper surface from the focused modules.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import storage
for name in ['ensure_agenda_dirs', 'task_file_path', 'dag_file_path', 'task_log_file_path']:
    assert hasattr(storage, name), name
assert hasattr(storage, 'normalize_task_record') or hasattr(storage, 'normalize_task')
assert hasattr(storage, 'normalize_dag_record') or hasattr(storage, 'normalize_task_dag_record') or hasattr(storage, 'normalize_dag')
for name in ['serialize_task_record', 'parse_task_record', 'load_all_tasks', 'load_task', 'next_task_id']:
    assert hasattr(storage, name), name
assert hasattr(storage, 'write_task') or hasattr(storage, 'save_task')
for name in ['serialize_dag_record', 'parse_dag_record', 'load_dag', 'write_dag']:
    assert hasattr(storage, name), name
assert hasattr(storage, 'write_task_log') or hasattr(storage, 'create_task_log') or hasattr(storage, 'append_task_log_entry')

human_checks: Treat storage facade thinness as an architectural judgment call; avoid turning it into brittle grep policing.
The focused modules should own paths, schemas, task storage, DAG storage, and log storage concerns even if the facade re-exports them.
